items.title: Hollinger POTD Kirk Snyder. Nevada G was drafted 16th by UTA. Good athlete scored 5 ppg as a rook but irked management w/trash talk & fights. Traded to NO in 05-06 had peak 8 ppg. Bounced to HOU & MIN thru 08. Had legal issues but worked them out & played abroad thru 2011.

items.title: June 3, 1998: NBA on NBC's intro for Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the Chicago Bulls and Utah Jazz.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 3, 1998: NBA on NBC's intro for Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the Chicago Bulls and Utah Jazz.

The Jazz won in overtime, 88-85. John Stockton had 24 PTS (9-12 FG)/8 AST and Karl Malone had 21 PTS/14 REB/2 BLK for Utah. Chicago's Michael Jordan scored 33 PTS (13-29 FG).

items.title: Under the old rules, let’s say the Jazz lose in the play-in next season, the Grizzlies would have 2% - 9% odds at a top-4 pick and 0% odds at picks 5-10
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Under the old rules, let’s say the Jazz lose in the play-in next season, the Grizzlies would have 2% - 9% odds at a top-4 pick and 0% odds at picks 5-10

Under new rules, the Grizzlies would have 0% odds of a top-5 pick but 15% - 28% odds at the sixth pick and 35% - 59% odds at landing a pick 6-10

items.title: Benson POTD Billy Donovan. Providence PG broke out as senior (20 ppg 7 apg) & drafted 68th by UTA in 87. Cut in camp & hooked up w/NY as a backup (2.4 ppg in 44 gms). Tried to make UTA again in 88-89 but waived. Went into coaching in 89 & has been ever since.

items.title: I'm generally supportive of NBA's lottery reform measures. But barring Memphis from collecting a (potential) top-5 pick from Utah in 2027 is objectively ... bonkers. Jazz traded the pick. Ergo, they can't benefit from tanking. Ergo, this wrinkle serves no clear purpose. What are
items.author: Howard Beck
items.body_excerpt: I'm generally supportive of NBA's lottery reform measures. But barring Memphis from collecting a (potential) top-5 pick from Utah in 2027 is objectively ... bonkers. Jazz traded the pick. Ergo, they can't benefit from tanking. Ergo, this wrinkle serves no clear purpose. What are we doing here, NBA?

items.title: Cavaliers shot 69.7% on 2s in Game 3. If you round up, Cavs are 4th team in 3-point era to lose a playoff game and hit at least 70% of their 2s. Coincidentally, Donovan Mitchell was on the first team to do it, and lost to Jalen Brunson. Jazz lost to Mavericks in Game 3 of their 2
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Cavaliers shot 69.7% on 2s in Game 3. If you round up, Cavs are 4th team in 3-point era to lose a playoff game and hit at least 70% of their 2s. Coincidentally, Donovan Mitchell was on the first team to do it, and lost to Jalen Brunson. Jazz lost to Mavericks in Game 3 of their 2022 1st round series
